name: Comeketo-Owned Venue (Hall + Catering + DJ Bundle)
status: ambiguous — surfaced by sales deck, unverified operationally
live_band: Unknown live-status. The Welcome-Rodrigo deck (slide 48) says 'THIS VERY VENUE — Officially Opens this Spring! 50-100 People — Ask us about options for Hall+Catering+DJ.' The deck is the 2026 version, so 'this Spring' reads as Spring 2026 (now underway as of 2026-04-22). No corroborating operational evidence has landed in the bedrock yet — no venue listing on the venue_partners roster (because it wouldn't be there — it's Comeketo's own), no booking rows in the cash-flow sheet attributed to an owned hall, no scheduling rows in the kitchen schedule that name it.
next_move: Ask Rodrigo directly: (1) Is the owned venue live as of April 2026? (2) Is the venue 'VirtualKitchenhall' — the sibling brand with its own domain and mailbox owning several Comeketo ops sheets? The VirtualKitchenhall naming (Kitchen + Hall) is a strong overlap with the slide-48 'Hall+Catering+DJ' offer. (3) If live, what's the address, who runs bookings, what's the rental-vs-bundled pricing? Rodbot cannot surface this offer to prospects until answers land.
blockers: Venue address not present anywhere in indexed files.
Venue name not explicitly given in the deck — referred to as 'THIS VERY VENUE' (the tasting-room itself?) or the sibling-brand VirtualKitchenhall?
No operational evidence (bookings, staffing, rental calendar) to confirm Spring 2026 opening actually happened on schedule.
Bundled-pricing structure (Hall+Catering+DJ) not documented — what's the rate, what's included, what upsells.
DJ component — is this the DJ Smokey partnership ($495 starter package from slide 54-68) or a separate Comeketo-owned DJ resource? The deck's bundle offer suggests pre-negotiated inclusion.
context:
The Welcome-Rodrigo deck's slide 48 reveals that Comeketo is not only a catering service but also a venue operator — or is about to be. The bundled 'Hall+Catering+DJ' offer at tasting-day is a material competitive moat: couples arriving at the tasting are shown a venue they could book on the spot, with catering and DJ pre-negotiated. This is a materially different business posture than 'we cater at your chosen venue.' The venue reveals at tasting, so it's not an inbound-pitch topic — it's a tasting-room conversion play.

The strongest inferential thread connects this project to 'VirtualKitchenhall' (the sibling brand). VirtualKitchenhall.com is a Comeketo-adjacent domain Rodrigo owns through the same GoDaddy account. Multiple Comeketo ops sheets (IG Insight Weekly Monitoring, Comeketo Venue Leads) are owned by office@virtualkitchenhall.com. The name 'Virtual Kitchen Hall' parses as 'kitchen + hall' — a physical hall with kitchen infrastructure, which fits the slide-48 offering perfectly. If VirtualKitchenhall IS the owned venue, several sibling-brand references across the corpus re-interpret: it's not a separate venture, it's Comeketo's venue layer with its own identity for ops/legal reasons.
hypothesis: (Speculative, pending Rodrigo confirmation.) The Comeketo-owned venue is operationally the VirtualKitchenhall site. Spring 2026 opening means Q2 2026 is the soft-launch window. The 'Hall+Catering+DJ' bundle is the tasting-day sales moat — couples who tour the tasting venue can book the venue + catering + DJ + price-lock all at once. If this pattern is live, it changes the playbook: a tasting-day close isn't just catering, it's a venue close.
metrics_to_watch: Bookings that include Hall+Catering+DJ bundle vs catering-only — if the venue is live, bundle penetration is the KPI that matters.
Tasting conversion rate delta pre-venue vs post-venue — if the slide-48 moment is real, tasting conversions should lift.

Cross-brand cost allocation — VirtualKitchenhall M365 seat ($22.99/yr domain + mailbox) is infra. A live venue adds operational cost (rent/mortgage, utilities, cleaning, event staff) — not yet in any indexed financial file.
DJ Smokey bookings attached to owned-venue events vs other-venue events — tells us whether Smokey is the bundled DJ or whether Comeketo has a separate in-house DJ resource.
notes: Critical unanswered question flagged in brand_welcome_rodrigo.json already — this project record makes the gap explicit and owner-addressable rather than buried in a knowledge-file footnote.
The sibling-brand VirtualKitchenhall linkage is a hypothesis, not a confirmed fact. Do not commit the bedrock to the linkage until Rodrigo confirms.

Rodbot red line until this project resolves: do NOT volunteer 'we have our own venue' in any B2C or B2B draft. The sales deck uses it as a tasting-day reveal; Rodbot outbound comes before tasting. Mentioning it early (a) gives up the tasting-day reveal moment, (b) risks claiming something operationally unverified.

If the venue IS live, this project graduates to 'active' with named ownership (probably Rodrigo + Camila on partnerships + whoever runs venue bookings) and gets a dedicated knowledge file for pricing/calendar/capacity specifics — essentially the owned-venue version of venue_partners.json.

sources.diff: Project created to track the owned-venue ambiguity surfaced by slide 48 of the Welcome-Rodrigo deck. VirtualKitchenhall sibling-brand linkage hypothesized as the probable operational identity based on domain/sheet-ownership evidence from the infrastructure inventory.
